content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

May 15, 1996

Ms. Lisa Hogeboom
Program Manager
West Contra Costa Transportation
Advisory Committee
One Alvarado Square
San Pablo, California 94806

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-127

Dear Ms. Hogeboom:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict-of-interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

Are boardmembers of the West Contra Costa Transportation
Advisory Committee (the "JPA") public officials subject to the
conflict of interest and disclosure provisions of the Act? Does
the JPA need to adopt a conflict-of-interest code?

CONCLUSIONS

Yes. Because the JPA possesses governmental decisionmaking
authority with respect to contracts, its boardmembers are public
officials subject to the conflict-of-interest and disclosure
provisions of the Act. The JPA should adopt a conflict-of-
interest code.

FACTS

The West Contra Costa County Transportation Advisory
Committee is a joint powers agency created in 1990. The members
of the JPA include the cities of San Pablo, Hercules, El Cerrito,
Pinole, and Richmond; Contra Costa County; the Alameda-Contra

__________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. References are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations
appear 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18995.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-96-127
Page 2


Costa Transit District; the San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit
District; and the Western Contra Costa County Transit Authority.
The JPA's board consists of ten members, appointed by the various
member cities, county and transit districts.

     In November of 1988, the voters of Contra Costa County
approved Measure "C" which established and funded a transportation
agenda for the county.  The JPA was formed to create a
transportation advisory committee which will advance the interests
of West Contra Costa County communities with regard to
transportation issues in general, and the use of Measure "C" funds
in particular.

     The JPA has a budget of approximately $95,000 made up of
contributions from the member cities, county, and transit
districts.  The finance division manager of the city of San Pablo
is designated to receive and be responsible for the JPA's funds.

     The JPA has a two-person professional staff and one part-time
clerical support position.  It also has two permanent contractor
slots for a transportation planning consultant and a consultant
who records the minutes of board meetings.

                              ANALYSIS

     The Act prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making, or in any way attempting to influence a
governmental decision in which the official knows or has reason to
know he or she has a financial interest.  (Section 87100.)

     The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act apply only to
"public officials."  A public official is defined as every member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government
agency.  (Section 82048.)  For purposes of the Act, each powers
authority is considered a local government agency.  (Elliot Advice
Letter, No. I-90-197.)  Regulation 18700(a)(1) provides that
"member" includes, but is not limited to, salaried or unsalaried
members of boards or commissions with decisionmaking authority.  A
board or commission possesses decisionmaking authority whenever:

          (A)  It may make a final governmental decision;

          (B)  It may compel a governmental decision; or it
may prevent a governmental decision either by reason of
an exclusive power to initiate the decision or by reason
of a veto which may not be overriden; or

          (C)  It makes substantive recommendations which
are, and over an extended period of time have been,
regularly approved without significant amendment or
modification by another public official or governmental
agency.

File No. A-96-127
Page 3


If the JPA meets any of the tests of Regulation
18700(a)(1)(A), (B) or (C), it possesses decisionmaking authority,
and its boardmembers are deemed public officials subject to the
conflict-of-interest requirements of the Act.

In the Fellows Advice Letter, No. I-91-395, we advised that
since the Irvine Transportation Authority had decisionmaking
authority with respect to contracts and the investment of funds
collected from fares, fees, and tolls, it was a decisionmaking
body under the Act, and its public officials subject
to the disclosure and disqualification provisions of the Act.

Paragraph 6 of the West Contra Costa Transportation Advisory
Committee's Joint Exercise of Powers Agreement provides that the
JPA has the power to do the following: to adopt a work program
and a budget for the JPA; to make and enter into contracts to
apply for and accept grants, advances, and contributions; to
employ or contract for the services of agents, consultants,
engineers, attorneys, and such other persons or firms as it deems
necessary to carry out the objectives of the joint powers
agreement; to conduct studies; to develop and administer a
regional program to encourage the use of alternatives to single
occupant commute travel; to periodically review transportation
plans; and to incur debts, liabilities, and obligations.

Because the JPA has the decisionmaking power to enter into
contracts, and to employ or contract for the services of
consultants, engineers, or attorneys, boardmembers of the JPA are
considered public officials under the Act.

Pursuant to Section 87300, the JPA should adopt a conflict of
interest code. Enclosed is a packet of information concerning
adoption of a code. The JPA's board members should file
statements of economic interests on Form 730 and are subject to
the Act's disqualification provisions, described in the enclosed
guide to the Political Reform Act.

I trust this answers your question. If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Hyla P. Wagner
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures
SGC:HPW:ak
